title: Pleioluma crebrifolia
taxonomy: Pleioluma crebrifolia is classified within the family Sapindaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the soapberry family. This classification places Pleioluma crebrifolia in the order Sapindales. As a member of the Sapindaceae family, Pleioluma crebrifolia is also classified as belonging to the class Magnoliopsida, which includes flowering plants that produce fruit with a single seed. The family Sapindaceae is further classified within the clade Rosids, which includes a large and diverse group of flowering plants. At the highest taxonomic level, Pleioluma crebrifolia is classified within the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Pleioluma crebrifolia, a plant native to tropical regions, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. Its leaves and bark are used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. In some cultures, the plant is also used to treat digestive problems, such as diarrhea and dysentery. Additionally, Pleioluma crebrifolia has been used to treat skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of its medicinal uses.
